    23. Free VS Paid
</h2>

  <!-- DEBUG: attachment.id=31153339, kind=text -->

<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p>Creators often struggle with the notion of FREE vs. PAID. </p><p>I had to come up with a solution for this problem after a few months of launching lastnightsparty. Later I found out that there was a name for what I was using:</p><p><strong>Value Ladders</strong></p><p>This is how you can create one for yourself.</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>FIRST, MAKE A MASTER LIST OF ALL YOUR OFFERINGS AS A CREATIVE</strong></h1><p>You need to write down everything you do as a creator that has value.</p><h3>Your creations themselves.</h3><h3>The creations you do for others (commisions, campaigns, one-on-ones)</h3><h3>Teaching your knowledge.</h3><h3>Giving a peek inside your world.</h3><p>Write down everything that you can think of. Even if you're not doing it yet.</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>THEN, SPLIT THE MASTER LIST INTO TWO LISTS: SCALABLE AND NON-SCALABLE</strong></h1><p>Scalable is a business conept to describe anything that you do that can reach more and more people without requiring more effort from you. Like playing a show: whether it's 5 people or 100, it's the same effort. That makes that activity scalable.</p><p>On the other hand... Shooting a campaign for a brand is NOT scalable because it requires you to do something unique, for one person.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>THE SCALABLE LIST:</strong></p><p>Put everything you can think of that you do that can easily be done / shared with 1 or 1M people without any extra effort on your part.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>THE NON-SCALABLE LIST</strong></p><p>Move all the things on the master list that AREN'T scalable to this list.</p><p>Things that require more time for you to do...</p><p>Or they require you to be physically there because you can't outsource it to anyone else etc... </p><p>Or anything that is a 1 on 1 service...</p><p>Or special appearance...</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>FREEMIUM LEVEL</strong></h1><p>Take a good look at the things you put on the SCALABLE LIST and figure out which things on that list should be free and which things should be paid for.</p><p>Start a new list for those things called:</p><p>THE FREEMIUM LEVEL</p><p>For example: Instagram should be on the FREEMIUM LIST.</p><p>This is a popular way for creators of giving their audience a piece of what they do for FREE.</p><p>Or in Erika's case (question above) it could be doing a weekly "Zoom workout" class.</p><p>What you will be able to achieve with the FREEMIUM LEVEL:</p><h3>1. Stay creative because creativity is like breathing to you.</h3><h3>2. Try new things out constantly</h3><h3>3. Give people a TASTE of what you do</h3><h3>4. Attract people to the KILLER PRODUCTS LEVEL (but you should be totally fine for them to stay at this level too. 'Cause sometimes those 'static' fans still attract other fans that "level up").</h3><p>The next level... all the stuff that is scalable, but that you will charge for.</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>KILLER PRODUCTS LEVEL</strong></h1><p>What you will achieve with the KILLER PRODUCTS LEVEL:</p><h3>1. Needs to be inexpensive. This is how you figure out who your buyers are.</h3><h3>2. Needs to be AMAZING. Way more value than whatever you decide to charge. Change people's lives. Overdeliver.</h3><h3>3. Attract people to the next level (but you're totally fine if they stay at this level too).</h3><p>This is where musicians can make money on merch.</p><p>Producers can make audio packs.</p><p>Artists can teach classes.</p><p>You'll notice that any of these things could have been on the freemium level too. But the key is to officially separate them in your mind and in your presentation so the people in your audience know all the boundaries. And once you set these boundaries, stick to them. That way you never have to feel bad. And your audience never feels cheated.</p><p>But where you draw the line is up to you.</p><p>For example, Erika could have decided to put "Zoom workouts" on this level instead.</p><p>She can charge people to be part of the class for a very small fee and overdeliver with a great product. But then she also could turn around and make "3 first Zoom workout classes" her FREEMIUM LEVEL. </p><p><br></p><h1><strong>EXCLUSIVE LEVEL</strong></h1><p>What you will achieve with the EXCLUSIVE LEVEL:</p><h3>1. Give people unparalleled access to you.</h3><h3>2. Share one-time only experiences.</h3><h3>3. Make the most amount of money at a time.</h3><p>For musicians that may be playing live.</p><p>For photographers that's shooting a campaign for a brand.</p><p>For a trainer that's one-on-one coaching.</p><p>Don't get me wrong. Before you can charge premium dollar for these things you'll have to do versions of them for very small fees.</p><p>But use these moments to fine-tune your product.</p><p>And then use the results to create value for the other two levels as BONUS MATERIAL. Never give things from the exclusive level for free.</p><p>If there isn't money exchanged, there still has to be some sort of trade to establish your value.</p><p>Train yourself to always find the trade.</p><p>You're on the rise.</p><p>Value yourself.</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>WELCOME TO YOUR NEW BUSINESS MODEL</strong></h1><p>So that you can set up realistic financial expectations for yourself, start to think of your different levels like this:</p><h3>What you do for the FREEMIUM LEVEL will attract 5% of people to pay for the KILLER PRODUCT LEVEL.</h3><h3>And the KILLER PRODUCT LEVEL will attract 5% of people to pay for the EXCLUSIVE LEVEL.</h3><p>With those figures in mind you can calculate how many people you have to reach (at a 5% conversion rate) in order to have a viable business.</p><p>Are you ready for the hustle it will require?</p><p>Girls that use ONLYFANS.COM have mastered this business model. They use Instagram for free stuff to attract people. And then their next level is the monthly subscription. And then the most dedicated fans pay premium prices for the private stuff.</p><p>Genius.</p><p>But don't forget this fun "game-changing" fact...</p><p>Sometimes your mid-level KILLER PRODUCTS become the cash cow. The mid-level income source can surpass EXCLUSIVE even though you charge more for EXCLUSIVE.</p><p>How?</p><p>Well, that's the very nature and reward of a popular scalable product.</p><p>That's exactly what happened to Metallica and every other metal band in the world.</p><p>They make more on merchandise than they do playing live. People want to own rock t-shirts even when they don't listen to the music. And there's no limit to how many t-shirts you can sell.</p><p>But there's a limit to how many shows you can play.</p><p>So pay attention to ALL the levels equally because they all play off each other.</p><p>That's right. Never disregard the FREEMIUM LEVEL. Give it energy. But the right energy. Know WHY you're doing things for free. Review the 4 reasons for FREEMIUM above.</p><p>Sidenote: That's why creating THE ULTIMATE IG is essential. A vanity based IG won't cut if you want this business model to work. </p><p>Now that you understand the basics, we're going to tackle the concept of how to create a whole experience for your audience in the next module:</p><p><strong>WORLD BUILDING</strong></p><p></p>
</div>
